customer_anxiety_profile: Barry has a concrete, witnessed safety fear — he saw a neighbor's house catch fire, which he attributes to roof-mounted solar panels. This is not abstract skepticism; it is a specific event. He agreed to the appointment but the fear was deferred to the specialist, not resolved. He is also 60 years old and made a self-deprecating comment about his own lifespan relative to the panel warranty — the closer should be aware of his life-stage framing.
unresolved_concerns: Fire safety / roof-mount liability: Barry witnessed a neighbor's house fire and attributes it to solar panels. He asked directly who is liable if panels cause a fire. Matt deferred to the specialist without resolving the fear.
Hazardous materials disposal: Barry asked who is responsible for disposing of solar panels at end of life. Matt correctly deferred to the specialist but the question is on record.
Panel lifespan: Barry asked how long panels last. Matt estimated 30 years but declined to commit. Closer should confirm and tie to Barry's life-stage comment.
Government incentive expectation: Barry came in expecting government money. Matt corrected this but the closer should confirm Barry's expectations are reset before presenting the proposal.
sensitive_topics_raised: Witnessed neighbor house fire attributed to solar panels — this is the emotional core of Barry's resistance and must be addressed directly before the proposal can land
Life-stage comment: Barry is 60 and made a wry remark about not living to see the panels wear out — acknowledge with care, not dismissal
Bill of $1,079 — Barry read this aloud himself; it is a live pain point the closer can anchor on immediately
exact_promises_rep_made: Specialist will answer liability questions (who is responsible if panels cause a fire)
Specialist will answer panel lifespan question
Specialist will answer hazardous materials disposal question
Confirmation text will be sent before the appointment
Notes about Barry's questions have been entered in the system
closer_should_know: Barry's fire-safety fear is grounded in a real witnessed event — a neighbor's house fire he attributes to solar panels. He agreed to the appointment but this concern was deferred, not resolved. Address it directly and early in the proposal call, with specific warranty and liability language, before moving to the design. His $1,079 bill is the emotional anchor; his safety fear is the gate. Also note: Barry is on Dominion utility — closer should be prepared with Dominion net metering context for the proposal.
